notes: Gravity for the PACKING scenario. The literal packing-up evening. Boxes, tape, sharpie, the choices, the keep pile growing more honest by the hour. The bungalow is becoming, room by room, fewer rooms than it was. Mostly pressing STAGNATION (the inertia of a thing she has been packing for six weeks) more than DOUBT.

deck.title: another box of books
deck.flavor: from the living room shelves. the donate pile is taller, this evening, than the keep pile. you have, this hour, become slightly more honest about that.

deck.title: the tape runs out
deck.flavor: mid-seam on the seventh box. you pull a fresh roll from the kitchen drawer. the fresh roll has, for two years, been the fresh roll.

deck.title: the sharpie refuses to dry
deck.flavor: the label smears under your thumb. you write the word again. you write it slower. the second write dries.

deck.title: you stop to look at a photograph
deck.flavor: from a shoebox you did not, this morning, know was in the closet. anya in '21. you in '21. anya looks like the take you have not, this week, finished.

deck.title: the trash is on the curb
deck.flavor: the bin you wheeled out is, you note from the porch, already half-full. you are not, you note, the only one packing on this block.

deck.title: the donate pile is bigger
deck.flavor: than the keep pile. you note this. you do not, this hour, move anything from donate back to keep. the honesty is the gain.

deck.title: the kettle is, technically, donate
deck.flavor: it has, since '24, whistled off-pitch. somebody else would replace the burner. you have not. you do not, this evening, put the kettle in either pile.

deck.title: you can't decide about the chair
deck.flavor: the wicker on the porch with both arms. you sat in it three nights this week. you sat in the other one once. the broken-armed chair is, by use, the chair. the both-arms chair is the one you take.

deck.title: the mover left a voicemail
deck.flavor: confirming the truck for thursday. asking whether you have stairs. you do. you have not, on the call-back list, returned the call.

deck.title: the closet is not, today, going to be opened
deck.flavor: the boxes inside are, by canon, closed since november. you walk past. you have, in the four months, not opened it. you tape a new sharpie label to the door: LATER.

deck.title: the picture on the dresser
deck.flavor: you walk past the bedroom door. it is face-down. you note this without entering the room. the not-entering is, this evening, the position.

deck.title: the welcome mat
deck.flavor: in cursive. the embarrassing one. you bought it in '23 for a reason you have not, since, examined. you do not, this evening, put it in donate.

deck.title: mackenzie recommends a mover
deck.flavor: a phil-and-kenz approved guy from the parish. she sends his number. the number includes a louisiana area code you did not, this morning, know you needed.

deck.title: you write KEEP on a box you have not, this evening, decided to keep
deck.flavor: the act of writing the word is, you discover, the decision. you do not unpack the box to check.

deck.title: the bungalow is fewer rooms than it was
deck.flavor: by midnight. the rooms you have packed look, from the hallway, like other rooms. you have, by attrition, started to leave.
